name: GaugeFlow P5-D0.8 Contractive Quotient Flow Map v1
scope: The sole authorized follow-up to failed D0.7. It keeps the D0.7 multiscale sampler, model capacity, fixed sources, optimizer, duration, direct, rollout, and semigroup losses. The only new term is a quotient finite-difference contractivity penalty. D0.3--D0.7 artifacts remain immutable.

rationale.selection_rule: D0.7 phase-1 perturbation audit measured first-step amplification 3.5566, mean per-step amplification 1.1749, and 32-step perturbed-state RMS 0.05351 from a 0.001 quotient perturbation. Since amplification exceeds one, the D0.7 authorization selects the contractive/Lipschitz option, rather than local/global maps or EMA distillation.
rationale.method: For an analytic on-path state x_s and a graphwise-translation-quotiented finite perturbation xi of RMS 0.001, penalize positive excess of the map quotient distance ratio: mean_g max(0, d_Q(Phi(x_s+xi), Phi(x_s))^2 / d_Q(x_s+xi, x_s)^2 - 1)^2. This is a finite-difference 1-Lipschitz constraint in the physical translation quotient; it does not alter the map architecture or sampler.

literature.citation: Boffi, Albergo, Vanden-Eijnden, Flow map matching with stochastic interpolants: A mathematical framework for consistency models, 2024
Song and Dhariwal, Improved Techniques for Training Consistency Models, 2023

model.time_injection: unchanged D0.6 Fourier interval FiLM at every block

training.loss: L_direct + L_rollout + L_semi + L_contract, where the first three are unchanged D0.7 energy-normalized losses and L_contract is the registered unit-weight finite-difference quotient penalty

evaluation.teacher_forced_metric: mean direct analytic-state-to-endpoint quotient RMS over 33 grid times
